approach: Extract shared waitForShellReady utility using VS Code's onDidChangeTerminalShellIntegration event, replacing fixed 800ms setTimeout across all providers
decisions: Used event-based shell integration detection (onDidChangeTerminalShellIntegration) instead of polling, with timeout fallback for older VS Code versions
Kept Gemini initDelay as separate setTimeout after shell ready — it's for CLI startup, not shell readiness

T002.did: Created waitForShellReady utility with event-based detection and timeout fallback

T003.did: Removed getTerminalDelay() and unused Timing import from configManager

T004.did: Updated ClaudeCodeProvider to use waitForShellReady everywhere

T005.did: Updated all remaining providers to use waitForShellReady

T007.did: Audited extension.ts — all disposables properly registered

T009.did: README checked — no stale timing references

specify.key_finding: executeHeadless already uses shellIntegration API correctly but executeInTerminal/executeSlashCommand use a fixed 800ms setTimeout

plan.approach_summary: Extract shared waitForShellReady utility using VS Code's onDidChangeTerminalShellIntegration event
risks: VS Code < 1.93 lacks event API — fallback needed
